title: GUI doesn't allow for the same `Default Workers` values as the CLI
author: yammes08
body: In the GUI app, the lower bound for `Default Workers` is `1`, not `0/auto`, and the upper bound is capped at `32`. It would be great to allow for values from `0` to `64` to be set, noting that I *think* values higher than `32` will be respected if passed via `presets.yaml`; `0` is not respected if included in `presets.yaml`.

title: [GUI] Check progress bar took an eternity to start for more than 30Go torrent verification.
author: sammy44nts
body:
When verifying a torrent in **mkbrr-gui** _v1.19.0-rc.1_, the progress bar took forever to move from 0% after I clicked the **Verify** button.

Environment: Linux OS.
36 workers accessing data through sshfs

title: No gui builds in the github releases
author: Ryu481
body: Currently there is a GUI for mkbrr but you have to compile it yourself because the gui builds are missing in the downloadable releases. Could the gui builds be added to the github releases so that one can easier get the gui for mkbrr?

title: tracker.torrentleech.org limited to piece size of 24, while it should be 28
author: realnc
body: TL documents it very clearly: Piece length should be limited to 28, but mkbrr doesn't support that anyway, so it should be limited to 27. But **not** 24.

title: [Feature Request] Add tracker piece size rules for a tracker
author: bobcate
body:
For OE+ (`onlyencodes.cc`)

Rules are the same as `lst.gg` in [mkbrr/internal/trackers/trackers.go](https://github.com/autobrr/mkbrr/blob/main/internal/trackers/trackers.go) file.

```
Under 1GB file → 1MiB
Between 1–4GB file → 2MiB
Between 4–12GB file → 4MiB
Between 12–20GB file → 8MiB
Above 20GB file → 16MiB
```

title: [FR] settings: Target Piece Count
author: StingRaymond
body:
Hi, I’m using mkbrr, I like it.

I want to suggest a new feature in the settings: Target Piece Count (with a hard minimum for safety, cannot be 10 (unheathly) pieces total)
Example of target would be 600 pieces or 900
Sometimes I wish a torrent to have less piece for less load in the client and a desired target would allow more granularity.

If an option « Target Piece Count » cannot be added in presets I would be happy with an option (fewer/neutral/more pieces) who would weight in the calculation of the number of pieces

Thanks a lot for the great work

It would be nice if we could define a torrent file output folder in the presets as well.

title: Homebrew formula `mkbrr` still at 1.18.0
author: backstab5983
body:
Hi 👋

The Homebrew formula i is still pointing to `mkbrr` **1.18.0**:

- `brew info autobrr/mkbrr/mkbrr` → `stable 1.18.0`

Upstream has released **1.19.0** on GitHub (published ~1 day ago), but it looks like the GitHub Actions/bot that normally bumps the formula hasn’t opened a PR / updated the version yet.


Thanks!

title: (feat): Support directory exclusion patterns
author: BudgieBird
body:
Spent a while trying to figure out why `AAC*/**` in my presets.yaml wasn't excluding the AAC! folder from my Blu-ray rips. Turns out `exclude_patterns` only matches filenames, not paths, so directory patterns seem to be silently ignored.

  Looking at `torrent/ignore.go`, the pattern matching runs `filepath.Match(pattern, filename)` against just the base filename. Patterns like `AAC*/**` or `somefolder/` never match anything.

  The only directory exclusion that works is the hardcoded `@eadir` in `ignoredDirNames`, which checks path segments directly.

  Would be nice if either:

  1. User patterns could also match directory names (the `shouldIgnoreDir()` function already iterates segments, so checking user patterns there seems straightforward)

  2. Or just add common encoding junk folders to the hardcoded list - `aac` and `aac!` are the main offenders for Blu-ray work

  Option 1 is more flexible since everyone's got their own junk folders to deal with. But either would solve the problem.

  For now, I'm running a cleanup script before mkbrr, which works but feels redundant given that there's already an exclude system in place.

title: `entropy: true` in preset does not work as expected
author: gizeto
body:
**Expected**: Adds entropy to torrent file. Generates different hash every time and .
**Actual**: No entropy added to torrent file. Generates hash different from the original torrent once, all the following runs hash is the same. 

command:
```bash
mkbrr modify --preset test --verbose image.png.torrent

Info: Modifying 1 torrent files...

Torrent info:
  Name: image.png
  Hash: b38e9d966a5bcc2d1f3758c90c8ef3c361dde32d
  Size: 2.1 MiB
  Piece length: 32 KiB
  Pieces: 68
  Trackers:
    https://tracker.fake
  Private: yes
  Comment: Test torrent
  Created by: mkbrr/v1.18.0
  Created on: 2026-01-06 23:43:37 CET

Wrote test_image.png.torrent (elapsed 5ms)

mkbrr inspect -v test_image.png.torrent | grep -i entropy
```

version:
```
mkbrr version
mkbrr version: v1.18.0
Build Time:    2025-11-26T20:12:19
```
`presets.yaml`:
```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/autobrr/mkbrr/main/schema/presets.json
version: 1

presets:
  test:
    trackers:
      - "https://tracker.fake"
    comment: "Test torrent"
    entropy: true
```

It works fine if pass `--entropy` parameter:
```bash
mkbrr modify --preset test --verbose image.png.torrent --entropy

Info: Modifying 1 torrent files...

Torrent info:
  Name: image.png
  Hash: 5e5690eac815f5be1e6662f1eeb0b05eae2731ea
  Size: 2.1 MiB
  Piece length: 32 KiB
  Pieces: 68
  Trackers:
    https://tracker.fake
  Private: yes
  Comment: Test torrent
  Created by: mkbrr/v1.18.0
  Created on: 2026-01-06 23:55:44 CET

Wrote test_image.png.torrent (elapsed 1ms)

mkbrr inspect -v test_image.png.torrent | grep -i entropy       
  info.entropy: 9c9a941ff53f2f34950d0ac4fc7428b6d5ae7bd647f88a31d5ea04b1db6279cb
```

title: [feature request] GUI - add support for drag and drop
author: PunchDrunkL0ve
body:
Hi,

The GUI looks great! well done.

One thing that's missing is support for drag and drop.
ie when dragging and dropping an mkv file over the Source Path, it would be very convenient if the full path of that mkv file is picked up.

Same way, in the inspect tab, dragging and dropping a .torrent file over the 'Select a torrent file to inspect its contents' the full path of that torrent file is auto filled.

and the same for check torrent and modify torrent tabs.

So pretty much implement the same behaviour when a file is dropped on a terminal window in MacOS or a command prompt window in Windows. You get the full path of that file.

Many thanks.

PS please make resizing the main window stick and not reset every time you run mkbrr-gui. Thanks.

title: Issue with adding multiple trackers
author: pmarsden
body:
Having used this to create torrents with multiple trackers, I notice they are all being added to the same tier.

This means they are not normally used because clients only announce to one tracker per tier, the rest act as "backups".

i.e. they display in the client like this ;

```
Tier 1

    udp://open.stealth.si:80/announce
    Last Announce: Today 19:39:11 (got 5 peers)
    Next announce: 7 Minutes, 28 Seconds

    udp://tracker.opentrackr.org:1337/announce
    Last Announce: N/A
    Tracker will be used as a backup

    udp://explodie.org:6969/announce
    Last Announce: N/A
    Tracker will be used as a backup
```

Multiple trackers need to be added as one per tier.

title: workers option is ignored in presets
author: realnc
body:
When using this `~/.config/mkbrr/presets.yaml`:

```yaml
version: 1

default:
  workers: 4

presets:
  some_preset:
    trackers:
      - "https://some.tracker/a"
    private: true
```

mkbrr still uses 256 workers when doing `mkbrr create /path/to/content -P some_preset` on an AMD EPYC 7702P CPU. Setting `workers: 4` in the `some_preset` preset doesn't help. The other preset options work fine, it's `workers` that gets ignored.

mkbrr version: 1.16.0
Build Time:    2025-10-02T18:33:33Z

title: [feat] BitTorrent v2 format (BEP-0052)
author: Artoria2e5
body:
The [v2 format](http://bittorrent.org/beps/bep_0052.html) has been out for... 5 years now. Among other interesting features it gives each file a merkle-tree hash with a fixed block size, so the same file always hashes the same. It also makes it possible to re-download in 16 KB block granularity despite a larger piece size. That's about it. Oh it can be mixed with v1 in a "hybrid" torrent file containing ".pad" files on the v1 layer.

More approachable descriptions are found in https://blog.libtorrent.org/2020/09/bittorrent-v2/ and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Torrent_file#BitTorrent_v2.

Is it useful, like practically used by many people? Not really. I don't know of any FOSS implementation other than libtorrent 2.0, and plenty of qbittorrent users are actively avoiding lt20. The only thing I can say is that I am very impressed by how hashing is done in mkbrr and would like to see it take a stab at v2. The same-file-same-hash aspect is nice.

title: [Q] How can I create a .torrent file for each and every single mkv file inside a directory
author: PunchDrunkL0ve
body:
Hi,

OS: Windows 10

I'm looking for an easy command line to create a .torrent file for each and every single mkv file inside a directory besides doing it in batch mode.

Ideally something like: `mkbrr create -P profile -e Path-to-Directory\*.mkv`

Many thanks.

title: A couple of suggestions
author: zoostorm1
body:
or requests if you want to treat them as such.

1. Add -q as a shortcut for --quiet
I rarely use this, it just seems to be more standard - many other cli programs simply use -v, -q

2. Add an option (like -i, --info-only)
This will output just the Torrent Info section that verbose generates (on completion).

i.e. This ... 
```
Torrent info:
  Name:         MyShow.S02E07.1080p.x265-Group
  Hash:         e34af0a978d76e51e6de66e5169e4b837c6c5457
  Size:         500 MiB
  Piece length: 512 KiB
  Pieces:       1000
  Trackers:    
    udp://tracker1.org:80/announce
    udp://tracker2.org:80/announce
    udp://tracker3.org:80/announce
  Comment:      Test
  Created by:   mkbrr/1.14.0 (https://github.com/autobrr/mkbrr)
  Created on:   2025-07-31 21:07:25 BST
  Files:        6

Wrote /data/Uploads/MkbrrTest.torrent (elapsed 80ms)
```
The reason for this is that all my torrent generation is automated (and the output redirected to log files).
All that fancy coloured updating & progress stuff does not look or transfer well to log files, the info looks fine.
